verses.commentary: Keep on loving one another as brothers and sisters. Philadelphia (brotherly love) is ongoing command. Believers are family, not merely acquaintances. Love must continue, not grow cold.

Do not forget to show hospitality to strangers, for by so doing some people have shown hospitality to angels without knowing it. Abraham and Lot entertained angels unaware (Genesis 18-19). Hospitality to strangers may bring unexpected blessing. The stranger may be more than they appear.

Continue to remember those in prison as if you were together with them in prison, and those who are mistreated as if you yourselves were suffering. Empathy, not mere sympathy. Identify with suffering believers. Imagine yourself in their situation. Love bears one another's burdens.

Marriage should be honored by all, and the marriage bed kept pure, for God will judge the adulterer and all the sexually immoral. Marriage is honorable, not inferior to celibacy. Sexual purity within marriage is commanded. Sexual immorality brings divine judgment. God cares about sexual ethics.

Keep your lives free from the love of money and be content with what you have, because God has said, Never will I leave you; never will I forsake you. Greed is incompatible with faith. Contentment comes from God's presence, not possessions. Deuteronomy 31:6 quoted. God's promise of presence is basis for contentment.

So we say with confidence, The Lord is my helper; I will not be afraid. What can mere mortals do to me? Psalm 118:6 quoted. Divine help produces confidence, not fear. Humans can harm the body but not the soul. God's help matters more than human opposition.

Remember your leaders, who spoke the word of God to you. Consider the outcome of their way of life and imitate their faith. Past leaders who faithfully preached and lived the gospel are examples. Their completed lives (outcome) validate their teaching. Imitate their faith, not just admire it.

Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever. Immutability of Christ. He does not change. Past, present, future—He is constant. This grounds stability in changing times. Leaders come and go; Christ remains.

Do not be carried away by all kinds of strange teachings. It is good for our hearts to be strengthened by grace, not by eating ceremonial foods, which is of no benefit to those who do so. Warning against doctrinal novelty. Strange (xenos) teachings deviate from apostolic gospel. Ceremonial food laws do not strengthen hearts; grace does.

We have an altar from which those who minister at the tabernacle have no right to eat. Christian altar is Christ's cross. Levitical priests cannot partake of this sacrifice. Separation from old covenant system is complete.

The high priest carries the blood of animals into the Most Holy Place as a sin offering, but the bodies are burned outside the camp. Day of Atonement ritual: blood taken inside, bodies burned outside. Leviticus 16:27. The carcasses were ceremonially unclean and removed from the camp.

And so Jesus also suffered outside the city gate to make the people holy through His own blood. Jesus was crucified outside Jerusalem (John 19:17-20). This fulfilled the typology: He is the sin offering whose body was 'outside the camp.' His blood sanctifies; His location signifies rejection.

Let us, then, go to Him outside the camp, bearing the disgrace He bore. Call to identify with Christ's rejection. Leave religious respectability and cultural acceptance. Christianity is outside the establishment. Discipleship includes sharing Christ's reproach.

For here we do not have an enduring city, but we are looking for the city that is to come. No permanent home on earth. Earthly cities, even Jerusalem, are temporary. Believers are pilgrims seeking the heavenly city. This perspective frees us from clinging to earthly status.

Through Jesus, therefore, let us continually offer to God a sacrifice of praise—the fruit of lips that openly profess His name. New covenant sacrifices are not animals but praise. Continually (dia pantos) means ongoing, not occasional. Praise is verbal confession of Christ's name. Hosea 14:2 background.

And do not forget to do good and to share with others, for with such sacrifices God is pleased. Practical goodness and generosity are sacrifices acceptable to God. Faith without works is dead. Generosity is worship.

Have confidence in your leaders and submit to their authority, because they keep watch over you as those who must give an account. Do this so that their work will be a joy, not a burden, for that would be of no benefit to you. Obedience to spiritual leaders is commanded. They watch over souls and will answer to God. Submission makes their work joyful; resistance makes it burdensome, harming both leader and congregation.

Pray for us. We are sure that we have a clear conscience and desire to live honorably in every way. Request for prayer. The writer asserts clean conscience and honorable conduct. Leaders need prayer support.
I particularly urge you to pray so that I may be restored to you soon. Personal request: pray for his return. He desires reunion with the community. Prayer can affect outcomes.

Now may the God of peace, who through the blood of the eternal covenant brought back from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great Shepherd of the sheep. Benediction begins. God identified as God of peace. Resurrection is through (dia) the blood of the eternal covenant. Christ is the great Shepherd (Ezekiel 34; John 10). His shepherding is resurrection-based.

Equip you with everything good for doing His will, and may He work in us what is pleasing to Him, through Jesus Christ, to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen. Prayer for their sanctification: God equips (katartizō) and works in us. Doing God's will requires divine enablement. All is through Jesus Christ. Doxology: glory belongs to Him forever.

Brothers and sisters, I urge you to bear with my word of exhortation, for in fact I have written to you quite briefly. Appeal to receive the letter graciously. He considers this brief despite its depth. Word of exhortation (logos tēs parakleseōs) describes the letter's purpose: encouragement and warning.

I want you to know that our brother Timothy has been released. If he arrives soon, I will come with him to see you. Timothy was imprisoned and is now free. The writer plans to visit with Timothy. This suggests the letter was written while persecution was active.

Greet all your leaders and all the Lord's people. Those from Italy send you their greetings. General greetings. Leaders are distinguished from the congregation but both are 'the Lord's people.' Those from Italy suggests the letter was sent to Italy (possibly Rome) or written from Italy.

Grace be with you all. Final benediction. Grace is the summary word. Everything comes from grace, depends on grace, ends in grace. The letter begins with God's speech through His Son and ends with grace to His people.
